title: Laser Safety Fundamentals Assessment
description: Comprehensive assessment covering laser classification, hazards, controls, and safety fundamentals.

questions.question: What is the primary ocular hazard associated with visible and near-infrared laser wavelengths?
questions.explanation: The retina is the most vulnerable structure for visible (400-700nm) and near-IR (700-1400nm) laser wavelengths, as the eye's optics focus the beam to a tiny spot on the retina, concentrating the energy.

questions.question: Which laser class requires the most stringent safety controls?
questions.explanation: Class 4 lasers are high-power devices that can cause immediate eye and skin injuries, ignite fires, and even make diffuse reflections hazardous. They require comprehensive engineering controls and safety programs.

questions.question: What does NOHD stand for?
questions.explanation: NOHD (Nominal Ocular Hazard Distance) is the distance along the axis of an unobstructed laser beam at which the irradiance or radiant exposure has fallen to the level of the MPE.

questions.question: Who is responsible for overseeing the laser safety program?
questions.explanation: The Laser Safety Officer (LSO) is the designated individual with authority and responsibility to monitor and enforce the control of laser hazards. The LSO must have authority to halt unsafe operations.

questions.question: What is the Maximum Permissible Exposure (MPE)?
options: The maximum power a laser can legally produce
The level of laser radiation to which a person may be exposed without harm
The maximum distance a laser beam can travel safely
The highest optical density eyewear available
questions.explanation: MPE is the level of laser radiation to which an unprotected person may be exposed without adverse biological effects. It is wavelength-, duration-, and tissue-dependent.

questions.question: Which type of control measure is preferred in laser safety hierarchy?
questions.explanation: Engineering controls (enclosures, interlocks, beam stops) are preferred over administrative controls and PPE because they do not rely on human behavior to be effective.

questions.question: Class 2 lasers rely on what natural protective mechanism for safety?
questions.explanation: Class 2 lasers emit low-power visible light (≤1mW). The natural blink/aversion reflex (≈0.25 seconds) provides adequate protection under normal viewing conditions.

questions.question: What standard defines LSO qualifications and responsibilities in the US?
questions.explanation: ANSI Z136.1 (American National Standard for Safe Use of Lasers) is the primary US standard that defines LSO duties, laser classification, MPE values, and control measures.
